title: Lightly Emulsified Venezuelan Cauliflower Tagine
description: A winey and springy ovo-vegetarian dish featuring cauliflower, emulsified in a rich Venezuelan sauce, cooked to perfection in a ceramic tagine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large ceramic tagine, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the cauliflower florets and cook until they start to soften, about 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the red wine, vegetable broth, eggs, tomato paste, cumin, and paprika.
Pour the wine mixture over the cauliflower and onion mixture in the tagine.
Cover the tagine and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the cauliflower is tender and the sauce has emulsified.
Remove the tagine from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
